video:video.video:title: Conan O'Brien on keeping Oscars jokes timely
video:video.video:description: Oscars host Conan O’Brien says the speed of the news cycle means jokes written a month ago are already 'Eh.' (Mar. 16)

video:video.video:title: Behind the scenes at Flightradar's HQ in Stockholm as flights are disrupted
video:video.video:description: Flight tracking platform Flightradar24 allows users to watch aircraft movements in real time, using signals transmitted by planes and received by thousands of antennas across the globe.

video:video.video:title: Excavators in southern suburbs of Beirut clear debris after Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: It comes as the Israeli military said on early Monday it is striking more infrastructure belonging to Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon’s capital.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 16
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, March 16th: Trump says he demanded countries police Strait of Hormuz; Iranian strikes in Gulf region; Israel releases video of troops in Lebanon; Winter storm heads from Upper Midwest to East Coast.

video:video.video:title: Bark and Ride - the canine coach exclusively for dogs
video:video.video:description: A canine coach exclusively for dogs is gaining celebrity status in the north of England, UK. Billy's Bus is part of a dog walking service that picks up your pooch for a luxury ride before taking them out for a run in the countryside.

video:video.video:title: Boats capsize off US air base construction site on Okinawa, two dead
video:video.video:description: Two boats carrying 21 people capsized Monday off a controversial relocation site for a U.S. military base off Japan’s southern island of Okinawa.

video:video.video:title: Large plume of black smoke billows over Dubai airport after drone strike hits fuel tank
video:video.video:description: The airport has repeatedly been targeted by Iranian drones, causing interruptions to air travel. It has also been shut down for several days since the start of the war between US-Israel and Iran on February 28.

video:video.video:title: Oldest known whale recording could unlock mysteries of the ocean
video:video.video:description: Researchers say the discovery of the oldest known recordings of whale sounds could open up a new understanding of how the huge animals communicate.

video:video.video:title: Mezcal's popularity is booming. That comes with a growing environmental cost in Mexico
video:video.video:description: Production of mezcal in Mexico is booming to meet growing global demand. The boom has both created opportunities for producers and come with environmental costs.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine firefighters battle intense blaze after deadly Russian drone strike on Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian firefighters battled an intense blaze after a deadly Russian drone strike on Zaporizhzhia early Monday.

video:video.video:title: UK Prime Minister says Britain 'will not be drawn into the wider war' in Gulf
video:video.video:description: Keir Starmer spoke after U.S. President Donald Trump said he’d demanded U.S. allies send warships to open the key oil shipment route.

video:video.video:title: EU wants to know more before agreeing to Trump's Hormuz warship demands
video:video.video:description: European countries on Monday demanded to know more about U.S. President Donald Trump's plans for the war on Iran and when the conflict might end as they weighed whether to agree to his call to send warships to help shore up security in the Persian Gulf.

video:video.video:title: New study finds ultrasound could help save hedgehogs from road deaths
video:video.video:description: Could ultrasonic hearing save hedgehogs from becoming roadkill? New research shows they can hear noise at frequencies as high as 85 kilohertz, raising the prospect of specially-designed sound repellers to alert them to approaching vehicles.

video:video.video:title: Videos show aftermath of strikes on Iran's cyber police and space research center
video:video.video:description: The strikes on Sunday happened as Iran said US and Israeli strikes damaged about 43,000 residential and commercial units.

video:video.video:title: Shrapnel seen on roof in East Jerusalem from interception of Iranian missile
video:video.video:description: Shrapnel from the latest Iranian missile barrage fell on a roof in the east of Jerusalem on Monday. Another shrapnel also fell on the rooftop of the Greek Orthodox Patriarchate in Jerusalem, according to the Patriarchate.

video:video.video:title: How to get more miles out of every gallon
video:video.video:description: When gas prices spike, filling up gets more painful. But consumers can do something to stretch their fuel just a little farther: Change the way they drive.

video:video.video:title: Conan O'Brien not fazed by a celebrity audience
video:video.video:description: Academy Award show host Conan O'Brien says he's not looking out for famous faces in the Oscars audience to see if they like his jokes.

video:video.video:title: Distraught relatives mourn women and child killed in collapse of wall on tents for displaced
video:video.video:description: The three-meter-high wall collapsed over tents sheltering displaced people in Khan Younis in the Gaza Strip, the city’s Nasser Hospital said.

video:video.video:title: French ex-president in court for first day of appeals trial over conspiracy to finance campaign
video:video.video:description: Nicolas Sarkozy arrived in court on Monday for the first day of his appeals trial over a criminal conspiracy in a scheme to finance his 2007 election campaign with funds from Libya.

video:video.video:title: Jane Fonda urges film industry to 'fight back'
video:video.video:description: Jane Fonda urged the film community to “fight back” as she attended the post-Oscars Vanity Fair party.

video:video.video:title: Trump says chief of staff Susie Wiles has breast cancer but will keep working through treatment
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has been diagnosed with early stage breast cancer but will continue working during her treatment.

video:video.video:title: What to know about the Iranian Women's Soccer team
video:video.video:description: The Asian Football Confederation general secretary Windsor John told The Associated Press that the team’s departure Monday night was arranged by the Iranian embassy.

video:video.video:title: Israel says it expects greater global support on Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: Israeli ambassador to the UN Danny said Monday the closing the Strait of Hormuz is a problem for the entire world, not just Israel and the United States.

video:video.video:title: Coast Guard crew rescues person stranded on ice in Lake Michigan
video:video.video:description: U.S. Coast Guard rescues a person stranded on ice near Mackinac Island, Michigan.

video:video.video:title: Large part of intercepted Iranian missile removed near homes in northern Israel
video:video.video:description: Members of Shomrey Haaretz, a national volunteer organisation, said the cylindrical shrapnel measured approximately 5.5 meters (18 feet).

video:video.video:title: At least 7 wounded after rocket attack on northern Israel
video:video.video:description: Israeli rescue services said at least seven people, including two teenage girls, were wounded when fire engulfed two houses in the northern town of Nahariya.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 16
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, March 16th: Trump calls on allies to help reopen Strait of Hormuz; Israeli rescue services say seven wounded in fire after sirens warned of combined rocket and drone attack; Sean Penn meets with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy; 200 trainees poised to be first gr

video:video.video:title: Memorial service marks first anniversary of deadly night club fire in North Macedonia
video:video.video:description: The tragedy that paralyzed the town of Kocani left an irreversible mark on the collective memory of the small Balkan country.

video:video.video:title: Spring is coming but the weather didn’t get the memo
video:video.video:description: Technically, spring arrives Friday during the vernal equinox.

video:video.video:title: Bessent insists that if Trump were to delay China trip it would not be due to Strait of Hormuz pressure
video:video.video:description: The US Treasury Secretary said any delay to Trump’s month-end trip to Beijing would not be because of disagreements over the Iran war or efforts to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Storms cancel more US flights as TSA remains under pressure from partial government shutdown
video:video.video:description: Travelers react to flight delays and cancellations as storms sweep across the eastern half of the country. (AP video: Erik Verduzco)

video:video.video:title: Strike in Afghanistan by Pakistan kills at least 400, according to Afghan officials
video:video.video:description: Rescue crews were still digging bodies out of the rubble of a drug rehabilitation hospital in the Afghan capital Tuesday morning, after officials there said an overnight Pakistani airstrike killed at least 400 people at the facility.

video:video.video:title: Israel says it killed a top Iranian security official and the Basij force chief
video:video.video:description: Both security official Ali Larijani and Gen. Gholam Reza Soleimani were “eliminated last night," Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said in a statement.

video:video.video:title: Suspected suicide bombers hit Nigeria’s Maiduguri city, killing 23 and injuring over 100
video:video.video:description: Residents and emergency services earlier told The Associated Press that three explosions were reported in crowded places in Maiduguri, the capital of Borno state, including in a major market and at the entrance of the University of Maiduguri Teaching Hospital.

video:video.video:title: Migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean, so is access to information from authorities
video:video.video:description: Migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean, so is access to information from authorities

video:video.video:title: How Poland moved from poor to prosperous in just 13 years
video:video.video:description: Poland is now among the world's 20 largest economies. This is how it happened.

video:video.video:title: Trump says a former president had an Iran confession; aides to his predecessors deny recent contact
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said he spoke to a predecessor who had expressed regret to him about not attacking Iran when they occupied the Oval Office.

video:video.video:title: Chile's new far-right president launches work on border barrier
video:video.video:description: Chilean President José Antonio Kast on Monday began overseeing preparations to build a border barrier — part of his flagship campaign promise to block immigrants from crossing illegally.

video:video.video:title: Marine veteran charged for violent DC protest says Trump is wrong in Israel, Iran war
video:video.video:description: Marine veteran Brian McGinnis has been criticized for wearing his uniform during an anti-war protest that left him with a broken arm and facing federal charges.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians gather in southern Gaza hoping to collect cooked food from charity kitchen
video:video.video:description: Dozens of Palestinians, many women and children, gathered in southern Gaza with pots to get cooked food from a charity kitchen - a scene that has become a norm in the war-torn strip.

video:video.video:title: Argentina’s wineries face historic slump as consumption hits record low
video:video.video:description: Argentina's wine industry, once a thriving business, is facing its worst crisis in more than 15 years, with record-low domestic consumption, dwindling exports and low-yielding crops. ( AP Video by Victor R.

video:video.video:title: Tehran warns US against sending troops to Iran, saying it would drag them into 'quagmire'
video:video.video:description: Iran warned the U.S. against deploying troops on its soil, saying it would drag them into a "quagmire."

video:video.video:title: Video shows amur leopard cat stealing fish from otter
video:video.video:description: An Amur leopard cat steals fish from an otter in a video captured at a Russian Nature Reserve.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of strike that hit village near Sidon, Lebanon
video:video.video:description: A strike early Tuesday morning hit a village near the coastal city of Sidon. There were no immediate reports of casualties from the Israeli airstrike, but the Israeli military issued a warning before the strike and said Hezbollah was using the building without giving further details.

video:video.video:title: Eight injured in Russian strike on Zaporizhzhia
video:video.video:description: Eight people were injured Tuesday in a Russian strike in Zaporizhzhia which damaged a terminal of Ukraine's biggest private delivery company, state and regional officials said.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of migrants are vanishing in the Mediterranean and authorities won't talk
video:video.video:description: Migrants trying to reach Europe are vanishing in droves in what are known as “invisible shipwrecks” but governments responsible for search and rescue are withholding information about what they know.

video:video.video:title: Interceptions seen in the skies over northern Israel as air raid sirens sound
video:video.video:description: An eyewitness captured video of interceptions in the skies over northern Israel on Tuesday morning, as air raid sirens sounded following a projectile launch from Lebanon.

video:video.video:title: At least 7 wounded after rocket attack on northern Israel
video:video.video:description: At least seven people, including two teenage girls, were wounded after a rocket strike set two homes ablaze in Nahariya in northern Israel, Israeli rescue services said.

video:video.video:title: Why the Trump administration is targeting media watchdog NewsGuard
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration’s clash with media organizations now includes NewsGuard, a company that rates the credibility of news outlets. Here's what to know.

video:video.video:title: Zelenskyy claims Ukraine's military 'thwarted' Russian offensive planned for March
video:video.video:description: Zelenskyy claims Ukraine's military 'thwarted' Russian offensive planned for March

video:video.video:title: Trump says he thinks he'll have 'the honor of taking Cuba'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he may soon have “the honor of taking Cuba,” telling reporters he believes he could control the island “in some form” as Cuba faced a nationwide blackout.

video:video.video:title: Interceptions light up Baghdad night sky after earlier drone attack on luxury hotel
video:video.video:description: Interceptions lit up the night sky over Baghdad after an earlier drone attack on a luxury hotel on Monday.

video:video.video:title: Last protester in immigration detention after Trump’s campus crackdown has been released
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian woman who was the last person still in immigration detention after the Trump administration’s 2025 crackdown on pro-Palestinian activists was released Monday on $100,000 bond after a year in custody.

video:video.video:title: Island-wide blackout hits Cuba as island struggles with deepening energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Officials in Cuba reported an island-wide blackout Monday in the country of some 11 million people as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

video:video.video:title: Several killed as planned demolition of building in Nairobi goes wrong
video:video.video:description: A building collapsed during a planned demolition that went wrong Monday in the Kenyan capital of Nairobi, killing at least four people and injuring four others, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Meatpacking workers strike at Colorado's JBS-owned Swift Beef Company
video:video.video:description: About 3,800 workers are striking at one of the nation’s largest meatpacking plants, seeking higher wages and better healthcare.

video:video.video:title: Watch: 'I do believe I'll have the honor of taking Cuba,' Trump says
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he believes he will soon have “the honor of taking Cuba,” but declined to say what that would involve.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 17
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, March 17th: Trump says an ex-president said he wishes he had attacked Iran; Explosions heard in Dubai and Qatar; Island-wide blackout in Cuba; Utah woman who wrote book on grief convicted of killing husband.

video:video.video:title: Trump says congressman Neal Dunn would have been 'dead by June'
video:video.video:description: The president revealed the illness of a Florida congressman during and event with the Kennedy Center board.

video:video.video:title: A taste of Ireland — delivered: Mobile pubs roll into New England neighborhoods
video:video.video:description: Two Massachusetts brothers have built a fleet of tiny Irish pubs on wheels — miniature bars they tow to backyards, weddings and neighborhood parties across New England.

video:video.video:title: Utah woman who wrote a book on grief after husband's death found guilty of murdering him
video:video.video:description: A Utah woman was convicted Monday of aggravated murder after poisoning her husband with fentanyl and then self-publishing a children’s book about coping with grief.

video:video.video:title: TSA Union calls for Congress to end shutdown
video:video.video:description: Travelers are planning to arrive earlier at the airport to make up for longer TSA lines as the TSA Union calls for Congress to end the shutdown.

video:video.video:title: Int. Feature film winner Joaquim Trier on protecting children in conflict areas
video:video.video:description: Joachim Trier called for more collaboration when it comes to protecting children in conflict zones as he spoke backstage after winning the Oscar for best international film Sunday for “Sentimental Value."

video:video.video:title: Drivers in Louisiana face pressure at the pump as gas prices continue to rise
video:video.video:description: While gas prices continue to rise nationwide, drivers in Louisiana have mixed feelings about the pressures they face at the gas pumps. (AP Video: Stephen Smith)

video:video.video:title: A-list arrivals at Vanity Fair Oscar party
video:video.video:description: Jane Fonda, Nicole Kidman, Colman Domingo and more attended the star-studded Vanity Fair Oscars after-party, on Hollywood’s most glamorous night of the year. (March 16)

video:video.video:title: Fashion editor highlights Oscars red carpet looks
video:video.video:description: From feathered gowns to bold color and Old Hollywood glamour, stars turned the Oscars red carpet into a lively showcase of standout fashion.

video:video.video:title: St Patrick's Day dreams - stay in a converted lighthouse in Ireland
video:video.video:description: It's St. Patrick's day, and visitors to Ireland's rural coastline can find a number of unusual places to stay.

video:video.video:title: Residents of northern Israeli city are under fire from both Iran and Hezbollah, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: For more than a year and a half, Kiryat Shmona, the largest Israeli city on the border with Lebanon, was a ghost town.

video:video.video:title: Last protester in immigration detention after Trump’s campus crackdown has been released
video:video.video:description: A Palestinian woman who was the last person still in immigration detention after the Trump administration’s 2025 crackdown on pro-Palestinian activists was released Monday on $100,000 bond after a year in custody.

video:video.video:title: Video emerges showing aftermath of strike in Shahrak-e Gharb area of Iran's capital Tehran
video:video.video:description: Video emerged Monday showing the aftermath of a strike in the Shahrak-e Gharb of Tehran. The U.S. and Israel pummeled military targets in Iran’s capital, as the war in the Middle East raged on multiple fronts.

video:video.video:title: Baghdad residents express concern about economic impact of continuing Iran war
video:video.video:description: Residents in Baghdad expressed concerns about the prolongation of the U.S.-Israel war against Iran and its security and economic consequences on Iraq.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds killed in Pakistan strike on Afghanistan hospital treating drug users
video:video.video:description: Afghanistan’s deputy government spokesman said early Tuesday the death toll from an airstrike by Pakistan that hit a hospital treating drug users in the Afghan capital Kabul has increased to 400.

video:video.video:title: Budapest's vintage 'freight trams' celebrate 100 years in transit service
video:video.video:description: The vintage machines were introduced after World War I damaged much of the city's infrastructure. Known locally as mukis, they run on electricity provided by overhead wires and travel on Budapest's vast tram rail network, which is widely regarded as one of the busiest.

video:video.video:title: Fog City sizzles: San Francisco swaps sweaters for sunscreen in rare March heatwave
video:video.video:description: San Francisco is having an unusual March heat wave, with temperatures nearing 90 degrees, marking the city’s hottest start to spring in nearly 20 years.

video:video.video:title: Muskets crack, drums echo as Boston marks 250 years since British evacuation
video:video.video:description: The Evacuation Day commemoration featured Revolutionary War reenactment units, music and a wreath-laying.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 17
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, March 17: Top counterterrorism official resigns over war in Iran; Rubio calls for new Cuban leadership; Afghanistan says more than 400 dead in Pakistani airstrike on hospital; crowds brave cold for New York's St.

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike kills two Palestinians in Khan Younis
video:video.video:description: At least two Palestinians were killed Tuesday by an Israeli airstrike in the war-torn Gaza Strip, the Palestinian Red Crescent said.

video:video.video:title: More than 400 people killed in strike on Kabul rehabilitation hospital
video:video.video:description: Rescuers recovered more bodies from the rubble of a drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul on Tuesday after officials said an overnight airstrike killed more than 400 people in a dramatic escalation of a conflict between Pakistan and Afghanistan that is now in its third week.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: ACCT Philly, the city’s open-intake shelter, received a call Sunday about a dog stranded high above the ground on a SEPTA train platform.

video:video.video:title: Metero's sonic boom scares pups in Ohio
video:video.video:description: A suspected meteor that fell over Ohio on Tuesday shook homes and startled residents who heard a boom some compared to an explosion.

video:video.video:title: 'Spent my whole day with the Irish': Trump says at annual St. Patrick's Day Shamrock Bowl
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and Ireland Prime Minister Micheál Martin closed out a day of events marking St.

video:video.video:title: Nashville-based reporter for Spanish-language news outlet expected to be freed on bond after ICE detention
video:video.video:description: Estefany Rodríguez Florez' attorneys said that she will likely be able to post bond and be released as soon as tomorrow.

video:video.video:title: Warsaw Zoo opens 24-hour drop off point for injured birds
video:video.video:description: Warsaw's accident and emergency room for wounded birds treats around 80 cases a day, all found and brought in by locals. (AP Video: Rafal Niedzielski)

video:video.video:title: AP explains: top Iranian security official and Basij force chief killed by Israel
video:video.video:description: Israel said Tuesday it killed two senior Iranian security officials in a major blow aimed at further weakening the Islamic Republic’s leadership.

video:video.video:title: Paraguay becomes final South American country to approve Mercosur-European Union trade deal
video:video.video:description: Paraguay became the latest—and final—South American country to ratify the long-awaited trade agreement between Mercosur and the European Union on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Strikes hit Beirut's southern suburbs overnight
video:video.video:description: Two strikes hit Beirut’s southern suburbs early Wednesday, sending flames and smoke into the sky. (AP video by Zakaria Al Khatib)

video:video.video:title: Venezuela rallies past Italy and will face US for World Baseball Classic title
video:video.video:description: Aaron Judge says crowds at this year's World Baseball Classic are "bigger and better" than the World Series. The AP has been there to capture the atmosphere.

video:video.video:title: Two men found with hundreds of ants are charged with illegally dealing in wildlife in Kenya
video:video.video:description: A Chinese national and a Kenyan man were charged Tuesday with unlawfully dealing in wildlife species after they were found in possession of hundreds of live ants stored in specialized tubes.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Cuba capital struggle as islandwide blackout hits in deepening energy crisis
video:video.video:description: Officials in Cuba reported an island-wide blackout Monday in the country of some 11 million people as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

video:video.video:title: AP reports: Suspected suicide bombers target Nigeria’s Maiduguri city, killing 23 people and injuring over 100
video:video.video:description: Residents and emergency services earlier told The Associated Press that the explosions were reported on Monday night in crowded places in Maiduguri, the capital of Borno state, including at a major market and the entrance of the University of Maiduguri Teaching Hospital.

video:video.video:title: Suspected meteor falling over Cleveland could be seen from far away
video:video.video:description: A suspected meteor that fell from the sky over the Cleveland area shook homes and startled residents who heard a boom that some compared to an explosion.

video:video.video:title: Drone crashes in US Embassy compound in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Two drones were shot down by the defense system at the US Embassy in Baghdad on Tuesday, two Iraqi security officials said. (AP video by Ali Jabar)

video:video.video:title: Republicans push voting bill toward Senate floor
video:video.video:description: Legislation that would require proof of U.S. citizenship for new voters has become a rallying cry for President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Explosions rock Beirut as plumes of smoke fill the sky
video:video.video:description: Large explosions and plumes of smoke were seen in Beirut on Tuesday. Earlier in the day, Lebanon's army said five of its soldiers were hurt after a morning airstrike on the village of Kfar Sir, two of them being seriously wounded.

video:video.video:title: Vice President Vance hosts Ireland's Taoiseach, Micheál Martin, for St. Patrick's Day breakfast
video:video.video:description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance and wife, Usha, greeted Ireland's Taoiseach Micheál Martin and wife, Mary O'Shea at the White House Tuesday as part of St.

video:video.video:title: 'Your Rich BFF' gives money tips for couples
video:video.video:description: Finances are one of the most important conversations to have with your significant other, Tu said. Talking about money as a couple can be intimidating, but it’s important for your future.

video:video.video:title: Cuba is gradually recovering from an islandwide blackout, but millions are still without electricity
video:video.video:description: Large parts of Cuba were without power on Tuesday after its third blackout within four months underscored the island's deepening energy and economic crises and rising political tensions with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Iran war begets fertilizer and other supply cost worries for US farmers
video:video.video:description: Rising fertilizer prices and potential shortages from shipping disruptions due to the Iran war have farmers bracing ahead of the spring planting season.

video:video.video:title: Gunman killed and employee hospitalized after a shooting at a Georgia VA clinic, police say
video:video.video:description: Police in Georgia say they have responded to a shooting at a Department of Veterans Affairs clinic, where a suspect was shot and killed by police.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya on guarding her private life and 'protecting things that are special'
video:video.video:description: At the premiere of "The Drama," Zendaya spoke about guarding her private life and “protecting things that are special.” (Mar. 17)

video:video.video:title: 'Bridgerton' star Regé-Jean Page says being among peers in Hollywood is a ‘privilege’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Vanity Fair Oscar Party "Bridgerton" star Regé-Jean Page says being among peers in Hollywood is a "privilege," and that he's aware that his audience wants to see him in more rom-coms.

video:video.video:title: Illinois Lt. Gov Juliana Stratton wins Democratic primary for US Senate
video:video.video:description: Illinois Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton won Tuesday's Democratic primary for U.S. Senate, edging out two sitting members of the U.S.

video:video.video:title: At Las Vegas casino's shark tank, the sharks eat just as good as the tourists, dietician says
video:video.video:description: Becky O’Brien always wanted to work with ocean life, but she didn’t realize she’d be able to do the job in the middle of the Las Vegas desert.

video:video.video:title: Mary J. Blige celebrates her journey: 'It's uplifting to have a career like this'
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Vanity Fair Oscar Party, Mary J. Blige reflects on her three-decade career and says she’s still learning new things about herself. (March 15)

video:video.video:title: Israeli airstrike hits an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike has struck an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut. The Israeli army had warned residents in the building to evacuate about an hour before completely flattening it as day broke.

video:video.video:title: Trump angry with NATO allies over Iran
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump is expressing anger with NATO’s refusal to join his push to send warships to secure the Strait of Hormuz. (AP Video: Mike Pesoli)

video:video.video:title: France’s tallest urban bridge suspended between legs of Paris' Eiffel Tower
video:video.video:description: The temporary attraction allows visitors to walk between the monument’s east and west pillars suspended on a 40-meter-long, 60-meter-high netted bridge accessible from the first floor of Paris’ most famous landmark.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya on 'protecting things that are special'
video:video.video:description: Zendaya’s new ring has led many to believe she’s married to Tom Holland, but she isn't talking about it — and says she's intentional about "protecting things that are special."

video:video.video:title: Coast Guard crew rescues person stranded on ice in Lake Michigan
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Coast Guard rescued a person stranded on ice near Mackinac Island, Michigan, on Sunday. The crew aboard Coast Guard Cutter Mackinaw was deployed to bring the person aboard the vessel and receive medical care, returning them to stable condition

video:video.video:title: From cart to doorstep in a flash: Amazon says it’s speeding delivery up again
video:video.video:description: Amazon said Tuesday that it has started offering faster U.S. deliveries of selected products for a fee, including pantry staples, clothing, over-the-counter medications, cleaning supplies and electronics.

video:video.video:title: UN chief urges diplomatic push to end war in the Middle East
video:video.video:description: The UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres has asserted once more that “the war in the Middle East must stop."

video:video.video:title: Drone crashes in US Embassy compound in Baghdad
video:video.video:description: Two drones were shot down by a defense system at the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad on Tuesday, while a third drone crashed inside the embassy compound, two Iraqi security officials said.

video:video.video:title: UN expresses condolences after airstrike on a hospital in Afghanistan kills hundreds
video:video.video:description: A U.N country representative in Afghanistan on Tuesday expressed its "deepest condolences" to the families of those killed by an airstrike on a drug treatment hospital in Afghanistan.

video:video.video:title: Rubio calls for 'new people in charge’ of Cuba as Trump declares the island 'in very bad shape'
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is calling for "new people in charge" of Cuba as the island experiences its third nationwide blackout in four months.

video:video.video:title: The Colosseum polishes up for its next 9 million visitors
video:video.video:description: The Colosseum has a bright new look following a restoration using the same travertine marble of ancient Rome to recreate parts of columns from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Large crowds brave the cold for New York's St. Patrick's Day Parade
video:video.video:description: Thousands lined New York City streets Tuesday despite the cold weather for the city’s annual St. Patrick’s Day parade, one of the largest celebrations of Irish heritage in the country.

video:video.video:title: Trump says NATO is making a ‘very foolish mistake’ by not assisting US in Iran war
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump complains about NATO’s refusal to join the U.S. in the war, calling the decision “disappointing” and bad for the “partnership.”

video:video.video:title: UN expresses concern for fuel shortage in Cuba
video:video.video:description: U.N.'s office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs expressed concern that fuel shortages in Cuba are curbing people's access to essential services across the country, according to U.N.

video:video.video:title: Dog rescued from elevated train platform in Philadelphia
video:video.video:description: A dog is safe after a rescue from an elevated train platform in Philadelphia. Stranded on a narrow ledge high above around, the dog was saved by ACCT Philly with help from police and transit workers.

video:video.video:title: Mezcal's popularity is booming. That comes with a growing environmental cost in Mexico
video:video.video:description: Production of mezcal in Mexico is booming to meet growing global demand, creating opportunities for producers. It has also come with environmental costs.

video:video.video:title: Trump, Martin and Johnson honor '250 years of Irish American friendship' at annual luncheon
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump, Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin and Speaker of the House Mike Johnson honored the "250 years of Irish American friendship" during the annual Friends of Ireland luncheon on Capitol Hill.

video:video.video:title: Trump team applying pressure to media: Tell the war’s story the way we see it
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and his team are increasing the pressure on journalists to cover the war in the Middle East the way the administration wants.

video:video.video:title: Trump: Not yet 'ready to leave' Iran but predicts an exit in the 'very near future'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is "not ready to leave" Iran yet, but vowed an exit "in the very near future."

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy calls on UK parliament to help build up anti-drone defences
video:video.video:description: In a speech to members of the British Parliament, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy called for joint efforts in building up anti-drone defenses amid the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Jury deliberating in corruption trial of ex-FirstEnergy executives accused of $4.3 million bribe
video:video.video:description: Jurors in Ohio have begun deliberating in the corruption trial of two fired FirstEnergy Corp. executives charged for their alleged roles in the $60 million bribery scheme centered on a $1 billion nuclear plant bailout.

video:video.video:title: Cuba is gradually recovering from an islandwide blackout, but millions are still without electricity
video:video.video:description: Energy is gradually being restored after Monday’s nationwide power outage, but millions remain without electricity. At this stage, Cuba is running on fumes.

video:video.video:title: Fuel costs rise as Iran war continues to disrupt flow of oil
video:video.video:description: Drivers in the U.S. are now facing the highest prices they've seen at the pump in nearly two and a half years. (AP Video: Mingson Lau)

video:video.video:title: Kenya's capital experiments with giving local government workers menstrual leave
video:video.video:description: Nairobi County has introduced Kenya’s first menstrual leave policy for female government staff, aiming to boost health and productivity.

video:video.video:title: Building reduced to rubble after strike that hit central Beirut
video:video.video:description: An Israeli airstrike struck an apartment building in Bachoura, central Beirut, completely flattening it as day broke on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: How Muslims celebrate the Islamic holiday, Eid al-Fitr
video:video.video:description: The Muslim holy month of Ramadan draws to an end and Muslims around the world will soon start celebrating the holiday of Eid al-Fitr, a joyous time marked with congregational prayers and festivities that include family gatherings and new clothes.
